revision.text:
'''新竹神社'''（しんちくじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代 (台湾)|日本統治時代]]の[[台湾]]新竹州新竹市客雅（現 [[新竹市]]中山路）にあった[[神社]]である。
[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、[[北白川宮能久親王|能久親王]]・[[国魂|大国魂命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]を祭神としていた。
[[1915年]]（大正4年）に造営が開始され、[[1918年]]（大正7年）10月25日に落成し鎮座祭が行われた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に[[県社]]に列格した。[[1938年]]より規模を大きくする改築が行われ、[[1940年]]に完成、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）には国幣小社へ昇格した。[[第二次世界大戦]]終戦の後、廃社となった。
現在は社務所と参道等が残っている。かつての社地には中国大陸からの難民・密航者を収容する施設（新竹靖廬）が建てられており、社務所はその管理施設として使用されている。
==外部リンク== *[http://www0.hccg.gov.tw/japan/02/2-10/history16.htm 新竹神社]（新竹市役所・日本語）
[[Category:台湾の神社|しんちくしんしや]] [[Category:国幣小社|しんちくしんしや]] [[zh:新竹神社]]

'''新竹神社'''（しんちくじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代 (台湾)|日本統治時代]]の[[台湾]]新竹州新竹市客雅（現 [[新竹市]]中山路）にあった[[神社]]である。
[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、[[北白川宮能久親王|能久親王]]・[[国魂|大国魂命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]を祭神としていた。
[[1915年]]（大正4年）に造営が開始され、[[1918年]]（大正7年）10月25日に落成し鎮座祭が行われた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に[[県社]]に列格した。[[1938年]]より規模を大きくする改築が行われ、[[1940年]]に完成、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）には国幣小社へ昇格した。[[第二次世界大戦]]終戦の後、廃社となった。
現在は社務所と参道等が残っている。かつての社地には中国からの難民・密航者を収容する施設（新竹靖廬）が建てられており、社務所はその管理施設として使用されている。

少彦名命| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年）}} '''新竹神社'''（しんちくじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代 (台湾)|日本統治時代]]の[[台湾]]新竹州新竹市客雅（現 [[新竹市]]）にあった[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== [[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、[[北白川宮能久親王|能久親王]]・[[国魂|大国魂命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]を祭神としていた。 ==歴史== [[1915年]]（大正4年）に造営が開始され、[[1918年]]（大正7年）10月25日に落成し鎮座祭が行われた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に[[県社]]に列格した。[[1938年]]より規模を大きくする改築が行われ、[[1940年]]に完成、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）には国幣小社へ昇格した。[[第二次世界大戦]]終戦の後、廃社となった。 現在は社務所と参道等が残っている。かつての社地には中国からの難民・密航者を収容する施設（新竹靖廬）が建てられており、社務所はその管理施設として使用されている。神社跡は新竹市の市定古跡に指定されている。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www0.hccg.gov.tw/japan/02/2-10/history16.htm 新竹神社]（新竹市庁・日本語） {{DEFAULTSORT:しんちくしんしや}} [[Category:台湾の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] *[http://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E4%B8%AD%E6%AD%A3%E8%B7%AF_(%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E5%B8%82) 驛前大道] [[Category:現存しない建築物]] [[zh:新竹神社]]

少彦名命 |社格=国幣小社 |創建=[[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年） | 緯度度 = 24 | 緯度分 = 47 | 緯度秒 = 31.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 120 |経度分 = 57 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = TW |地図 = Taiwan }} '''新竹神社'''（しんちくじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の台湾|日本統治時代]]の[[台湾]]新竹州新竹市客雅（現[[新竹市]][[北区 (新竹市)|北区]]）にあった[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== [[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、[[北白川宮能久親王|能久親王]]・[[国魂|大国魂命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]を祭神としていた。 ==歴史== [[1915年]]（大正4年）に造営が開始され、[[1918年]]（大正7年）10月25日に落成し鎮座祭が行われた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に[[県社]]に列格した。[[1938年]]より規模を大きくする改築が行われ、[[1940年]]に完成、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）には国幣小社へ昇格した。[[第二次世界大戦]]終戦の後、廃社となった。 現在は社務所と参道等が残っている。かつての社地には中国からの難民・密航者を収容する施設（新竹靖廬）が建てられており、社務所はその管理施設として使用されている。神社跡は新竹市の市定古跡に指定されている{{Zh-tw icon}}[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[文化部文化資産局]]国家文化資産網。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 新竹神社]（新竹市庁・日本語） *[[:zh:%E4%B8%AD%E6%AD%A3%E8%B7%AF_(%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E5%B8%82)|驛前大道]] {{神道 横}} {{Taiwan-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんちくしんしや}} [[Category:台湾の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない台湾の建築物]] [[Category:新竹市の歴史]] [[Category:台湾の古跡|竹]]

revision.text:
{{神社|名称=新竹神社|画像=[[ファイル:1940 shinchiku jinja.jpg|250px|新竹神社]]
新竹神社|所在地=台湾新竹州新竹市客雅|祭神=能久親王
大国魂命
大己貴命
少彦名命|社格=国幣小社|創建=[[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年）|緯度度=24|緯度分=47|緯度秒=31.4|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N|経度度=120|経度分=57|経度秒=8.9|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E|ISO=TW|地図=Taiwan}}'''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[Hsinchu]] City, [[Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule. == Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] . == History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down. The shrine office and the [[Sando|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City. {{Zh-tw icon}}[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]]国家文化資産網 . == Sources == {{Reflist}} == Related item == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Taiwan]] == External link == {{Commons category-inline|Hsinchu Shrine}} * [https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 Hsinchu Shrine] (Hsinchu City Hall/English) [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]

少彦名命 |社格=国幣小社 |創建=[[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年） | 緯度度 = 24 | 緯度分 = 47 | 緯度秒 = 31.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 120 |経度分 = 57 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = TW |地図 = Taiwan }} '''新竹神社'''（しんちくじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の台湾|日本統治時代]]の[[台湾]]新竹州新竹市客雅（現[[新竹市]][[北区 (新竹市)|北区]]）にあった[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== [[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、[[北白川宮能久親王|能久親王]]・[[国魂|大国魂命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]を祭神としていた。 ==歴史== [[1915年]]（大正4年）に造営が開始され、[[1918年]]（大正7年）10月25日に落成し鎮座祭が行われた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に[[県社]]に列格した。[[1938年]]より規模を大きくする改築が行われ、[[1940年]]に完成、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）には国幣小社へ昇格した。[[第二次世界大戦]]終戦の後、廃社となった。 現在は社務所と参道等が残っている。かつての社地には中国からの難民・密航者を収容する施設（新竹靖廬）が建てられており、社務所はその管理施設として使用されている。神社跡は新竹市の市定古跡に指定されている{{Zh-tw icon}}[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[文化部文化資産局]]国家文化資産網。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[台湾の神社]] *[[:zh:%E4%B8%AD%E6%AD%A3%E8%B7%AF_(%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E5%B8%82)|驛前大道]] ==外部リンク== {{commonscat-inline|Hsinchu Shrine}} *[https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 新竹神社]（新竹市庁・日本語） {{神道 横}} {{Taiwan-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんちくしんしや}} [[Category:台湾の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない台湾の建築物]] [[Category:新竹市の歴史]] [[Category:台湾の古跡|竹]]

revision.comment: ([[c:GR|GR]]) [[c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[File:1940 shinchiku jinja.jpg]] → [[File:1940 shinchiku jinja torii.jpg]] [[c:COM:FR#FR2|Criterion 2]] (meaningless or ambiguous name)
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building|名称=新竹神社|画像=|image=1940 shinchiku jinja torii.jpg|250px|新竹神社]]
新竹神社|所在地=台湾新竹州新竹市客雅|祭神=能久親王
大国魂命
大己貴命
少彦名命|社格=国幣小社|創建=[[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年）|緯度度=24|緯度分=47|緯度秒=31.4|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N|経度度=120|経度分=57|経度秒=8.9|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E|ISO=TW|地図=Taiwan}}'''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[Hsinchu]] City, [[Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule. == Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] . == History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down. The shrine office and the [[Sando|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.{{Zh-tw icon}}[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]]国家文化資産網 == References == {{Reflist}} == Related item == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Taiwan]] == External link == {{Commons category-inline|Hsinchu Shrine}} * [https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 Hsinchu Shrine] (Hsinchu City Hall/English) [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]]

revision.text:
'''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[Hsinchu]] City, [[Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[Sando|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

[[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Shinchiku Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]]

'''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[Hsinchu]] City, [[Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[Sando|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

'''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[Hsinchu]] City, [[Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[Sando|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

revision.text: '''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, ]][[Hsinchu]]]][[ja: City, ]][[Taiwan]]]][[ja:. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.== Deities ==The deities worshipped at the shrine were ]][[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]]]][[ja:, {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, ]][[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]]]][[ja:, and ]][[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]]][[ja: .== History ==In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.The shrine office and the ]][[Sando|approach to the shrine]]]][[ja: are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] ]][[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]]]][[ja: 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>== References =={{Reflist}}== Other websites =={{Commons category|Hsinchu Shrine}}* [https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 Hsinchu Shrine] (Hsinchu City Hall/English)]][[category:Kokuhei Shōsha]]]][[ja:]][[category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]]][[ja:]][[category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]]]][[ja:]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]]]][[ja:]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]]]][[ja:]][[category:mm]]]][[ja:]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]]]][[ja:]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]]]][[ja:]][[category:mm]]]][[ja:]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]]]][[ja:]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]]]][[ja:]][[category:mm]]]][[ja:]][[category:dd]]]][[ja:]][[category:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]][[category:dd]]]][[ja:]][[category:Bump-01-21]]]][[ja:]][[category:Bump-01-21]]]][[ja:]][[category:bump 1-26]]]][[ja:]][[Category:Kk]]]][[ja:]][[ja:]][[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]]][[Category:qq]]]][[Category:qq]]]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[Category:qq]]]][[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]]][[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]]][[ja:]][[zh:%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]]][[ja:]][[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]]][[ja:]][[simple:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:

'''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, ]][[Hsinchu]]]][[ja: City, ]][[Taiwan]]]][[ja:. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.== Deities ==The deities worshipped at the shrine were ]][[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]]]][[ja:, {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, ]][[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]]]][[ja:, and ]][[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]]][[ja: .== History ==In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.The shrine office and the ]][[Sando|approach to the shrine]]]][[ja: are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] ]][[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]]]][[ja: 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>== References =={{Reflist}}== Other websites =={{Commons category|Hsinchu Shrine}}* [https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 Hsinchu Shrine] (Hsinchu City Hall/English)]][[category:Kokuhei Shōsha]]]][[ja:]][[category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]]][[ja:]][[category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]]]][[ja:]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]]]][[ja:]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]]]][[ja:]][[category:mm]]]][[ja:]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]]]][[ja:]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]]]][[ja:]][[category:mm]]]][[ja:]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]]]][[ja:]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]]]][[ja:]][[category:mm]]]][[ja:]][[category:dd]]]][[ja:]][[category:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:]][[category:dd]]]][[ja:]][[category:Bump-01-21]]]][[ja:]][[category:Bump-01-21]]]][[ja:]][[category:bump 1-26]]]][[ja:]][[Category:Kk]]]][[ja:]][[ja:]][[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]]][[Category:qq]]]][[Category:qq]]]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[Category:qq]]]]]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]]]]]][[en:Shinchiku Shrine]]]]]][[ja:]][[zh:%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]]][[ja:]][[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]]][[ja:]][[simple:Shinchiku Shrine]]]][[ja:

It was built during the period of Japanese rule.== Deities ==The deities worshipped at the shrine were ]][[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]]]][[ja:, {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, ]][[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]]]][[ja:, and ]][[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]]][[ja: .== History ==In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.The shrine office and the ]][[Sando|approach to the shrine]]]][[ja: are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. revision.text:
'''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[Hsinchu]] City, [[Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[Sando|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.The shrine office and the ]] [[en:Shinchiku Shrine]] [[zh:%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:

revision.comment: 26 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:新竹神社 up to rev 99996788
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=新竹神社 |画像=[[ファイル:1940 shinchiku jinja torii.jpg|250px|新竹神社]]
新竹神社 |所在地=[[日本統治時代の台湾|台湾]][[新竹州]][[新竹市 (日本統治時代)|新竹市]]客雅 |祭神=能久親王
大国魂命
大己貴命
少彦名命 |社格=国幣小社 |創建=[[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年） | 緯度度 = 24 | 緯度分 = 47 | 緯度秒 = 31.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 120 |経度分 = 57 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = TW |地図 = Taiwan }} '''新竹神社'''（しんちくじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の台湾|日本統治時代]]の[[台湾]][[新竹州]][[新竹市 (日本統治時代)|新竹市]]客雅（現[[新竹市]][[北区 (新竹市)|北区]]）にあった[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== [[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、[[北白川宮能久親王|能久親王]]・[[国魂|大国魂命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]を祭神としていた。 ==歴史== [[1915年]]（大正4年）に造営が開始され、[[1918年]]（大正7年）10月25日に落成し鎮座祭が行われた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に[[県社]]に列格した。[[1938年]]より規模を大きくする改築が行われ、[[1940年]]に完成、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）には国幣小社へ昇格した。[[第二次世界大戦]]終戦の後、廃社となった。 現在は社務所と参道等が残っている。かつての社地には中国からの難民・密航者を収容する施設（新竹靖廬）が建てられており、社務所はその管理施設として使用されている。神社跡は新竹市の市定古跡に指定されている{{Zh-tw icon}}[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[文化部文化資産局]]国家文化資産網。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[台湾の神社]] *[[:zh:%E4%B8%AD%E6%AD%A3%E8%B7%AF_(%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E5%B8%82)|驛前大道]] ==外部リンク== {{commonscat-inline|Hsinchu Shrine}} *[https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 新竹神社]（新竹市庁・日本語） {{神道 横}} {{Taiwan-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんちくしんしや}} [[Category:台湾の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない台湾の建築物]] [[Category:新竹市の歴史]] [[Category:台湾の古跡|竹]]

{{神社 |名称=新竹神社 |画像=[[ファイル:1940 shinchiku jinja torii.jpg|250px|新竹神社]]
新竹神社 |所在地=[[日本統治時代の台湾|台湾]][[新竹州]][[新竹市 (日本統治時代)|新竹市]]客雅 |祭神=能久親王
大国魂命
大己貴命
少彦名命 |社格=国幣小社 |創建=[[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年） | 緯度度 = 24 | 緯度分 = 47 | 緯度秒 = 31.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 120 |経度分 = 57 | 経度秒 = 8.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = TW |地図 = Taiwan }} '''新竹神社'''（しんちくじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の台湾|日本統治時代]]の[[台湾]][[新竹州]][[新竹市 (日本統治時代)|新竹市]]客雅（現[[新竹市]][[北区 (新竹市)|北区]]）にあった[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== [[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、[[北白川宮能久親王|能久親王]]・[[国魂|大国魂命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]を祭神としていた。 ==歴史== [[1915年]]（大正4年）に造営が開始され、[[1918年]]（大正7年）10月25日に落成し鎮座祭が行われた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に[[県社]]に列格した。[[1938年]]より規模を大きくする改築が行われ、[[1940年]]に完成、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）には国幣小社へ昇格した。[[第二次世界大戦]]終戦の後、廃社となった。 現在は社務所と参道等が残っている。かつての社地には中国からの難民・密航者を収容する施設（新竹靖廬）が建てられており、社務所はその管理施設として使用されている。神社跡は新竹市の市定古跡に指定されている{{Zh-tw icon}}[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[文化部文化資産局]]国家文化資産網。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[台湾の神社]] *[[:zh:%E4%B8%AD%E6%AD%A3%E8%B7%AF_(%E6%96%B0%E7%AB%B9%E5%B8%82)|驛前大道]] ==外部リンク== {{commonscat-inline|Hsinchu Shrine}} *[https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 新竹神社]（新竹市庁・日本語） {{神道 横}} {{Taiwan-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんちくしんしや}} [[Category:台湾の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない台湾の建築物]] [[Category:新竹市の歴史]] [[Category:台湾の古跡|竹]]

'''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[Hsinchu]] City, [[Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], {{ill|Kunitama Omikami|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto|ja|国魂}}, [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[Sando|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. == History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[Sando|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:1940 shinchiku jinja torii.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Shinchiku City|qid=Q11083992}}|coordinates={{Coord|24.79233|N|120.9525|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11084040]] Wikidata name: Hsinchu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[:en:Hsinchu|Hsinchu]] City, [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], [[:en:Kunitama|Okunitama no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[:en:Sandō|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Other websites == {{Commons category|Hsinchu Shrine}} * [https://www.hccg.gov.tw/jp/home.jsp?id=41&parentpath=0 Hsinchu Shrine] (Hsinchu City Hall/English)
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]]
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Hsinchu Shrine]][[ja:新竹神社]][[simple:Hsinchu Shrine]][[zh:新竹神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:1940 shinchiku jinja torii.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Shinchiku City|WD=Q11083992}}|coordinates={{Coord|24.79233|N|120.9525|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11084040]] Wikidata name: Hsinchu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[:en:Hsinchu|Hsinchu]] City, [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], [[:en:Kunitama|Okunitama no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[:en:Sandō|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Hsinchu Shrine]][[ja:新竹神社]][[simple:Hsinchu Shrine]][[zh:新竹神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Hsinchu Shrine]][[ja:新竹神社]][[simple:Hsinchu Shrine]][[zh:新竹神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Hsinchu Shrine]][[ja:新竹神社]][[simple:Hsinchu Shrine]][[zh:新竹神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:1940 shinchiku jinja torii.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Shinchiku City|WD=Q11083992}}|coordinates={{Coord|24.79233|N|120.9525|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11084040]] Wikidata name: Hsinchu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, [[:en:Hsinchu|Hsinchu]] City, [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Yoshihisa]], [[:en:Kunitama|Okunitama no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the [[:en:Sandō|approach to the shrine]] are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Hsinchu Shrine]][[ja:新竹神社]][[simple:Hsinchu Shrine]][[zh:新竹神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Hsinchu Shrine]][[ja:新竹神社]][[simple:Hsinchu Shrine]][[zh:新竹神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Hsinchu Shrine]][[ja:新竹神社]][[simple:Hsinchu Shrine]][[zh:新竹神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:1940 shinchiku jinja torii.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Shinchiku City|WD=Q11083992}}|coordinates={{Coord|24.79233|N|120.9525|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11084040]] Wikidata name: Hsinchu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinchiku Jinja''' is a shrine located in Kita District, {{ill|Hsinchu|en|Hsinchu|WD=Q249994|lt=Hsinchu}} City, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}}. It was built during the period of Japanese rule.
== Deities == The deities worshipped at the shrine were {{ill|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|en|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|WD=Q3197569|lt=Prince Yoshihisa}}, {{ill|Kunitama|en|Kunitama|WD=Q9692242|lt=Okunitama no Mikoto}}, [[Ōkuninushi|Okununishi no Mikoto]], and [[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] .
== History == In 1915, construction of the Hsinchu Shrine began and was finished on October 25, 1918. An enshrinement ceremony followed. It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach to the shrine}} are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>

It was later elevated to a prefectural shrine in 1920 and underwent renovations in 1938. The renovations were completed in 1940, and in 1942, it was promoted to Kokuhei Shosha. However, after the end of World War II, the shrine was closed down.
The shrine office and the {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach to the shrine|1=Sandō}} are the only remaining structures at the site. The former shrine grounds have been repurposed to house a facility for refugees and stowaways from China, known as Hsinchu Yasuro. The shrine office has been converted to a management facility for this purpose. The site has been designated as a municipal historic site in Hsinchu City.<ref>[https://nchdb.boch.gov.tw/assets/overview/monument/20010531000002 新竹神社殘蹟及其附屬建築] [[Cultural Assets Bureau, Ministry of Culture|文化部文化資産局]] 国家文化資産網 {{in lang|Zh-tw}}</ref>
